macro_segments.title: Opening Teaching: Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar on Wisdom of Elders and Example of Rehoboam

micro_segments.title: Baraita of Rabbi Shimon ben Elazar: If elders say demolish and youth say build
Explanation: Destruction of elders is constructive, construction of youth is destructive; Example of Rehoboam

macro_segments.title: Reading Schedule for Torah Portions Across Days of Week

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Where Shabbat morning reading ends, afternoon reading begins; Three-day and weekly continuity
Rabbi Meir's view: Separate stopping points; Rabbi Yehuda's view: Same aliyah repeated across days
Ruling of Rabbi Zeira in name of Rav Matnah: Halacha follows Rabbi Yehuda; Reason for not naming the Tanna

macro_segments.title: Procedure for Opening, Blessing, and Reading the Sefer Torah

micro_segments.title: Baraita: Open and look, close, bless, open and read (Rabbi Yehuda's view)
Rabbi Meir's view: Reason from Ulla regarding bracha separation; People should not think blessings are written in Torah
Rabbi Yehuda's counter-argument: No confusion regarding blessings unlike Aramaic words in Torah
Ruling of Rabbi Zeira: Halacha follows opening, blessing, reading without closing in between; Tosafot's view on lechatchila and bidi'avad

macro_segments.title: Sanctity of Tablets, Platforms, and Margins of Sefer Torah

micro_segments.title: Statement of Rav Shefatya in name of Rabbi Yochanan: Tablets and platforms have no sanctity
Rashi's interpretation: Tablets may refer to book covers; platforms to structures for reading
Tosafot and Rabbeinu Chananel: Tablets and platforms are blank spaces in Torah scroll; sanctity lost once removed

macro_segments.title: Proper Technique for Rolling and Tightening the Sefer Torah

micro_segments.title: Rav Shefatya in name of Rabbi Yochanan: Roller must position seam in middle when rolling
Reason: To prevent tearing of parchment at weak points; Application to both rolling and lifting
Further instruction: Roll from outside, tighten from inside; Rashi's explanation of one-person handling
Tosafot's alternative reading: Tightening refers to gartel knot placement; Rolling means standing behind for visibility

macro_segments.title: Importance of Gelila (Rolling) and Honors Associated with Torah Service

micro_segments.title: Baraita: If ten people read Torah, the greatest among them does gelila and receives credit equivalent to all
Rabbeinu Chananel: One who completes the reading gets credit; possibly final aliyah reader should do gelila
Clarification: Receives credit equivalent to all, not actually taking their credit away

macro_segments.title: Using Heavenly Voice (Bas Kol) as a Sign for Guidance

micro_segments.title: Rav Shefatya in name of Rabbi Yochanan: Permitted to use bas kol as sign when needed for decision-making
Rashi: Valid only if unusual sound (man's voice in city or woman's voice in field) and repeated affirmation (yes, yes or no, no)
Tosafot: Source from Jerusalem Talmud; Distinction between divination (forbidden) and signs (permitted)

macro_segments.title: Reading Torah with Trop and Learning Mishnah with Melody

micro_segments.title: Rav Shefatya in name of Rabbi Yochanan: One who reads Torah without trop and learns Mishnah without melody transgresses
Proof text: 'I gave them laws that are not good' - refers to improper transmission of traditions
Abaye's objection: Issue is not just poor voice quality but proper learning and transmission; Rav Masharshiya's teaching on scholars not learning together
Connection to chavrusa learning: Laws require discussion and critique to be properly transmitted and understood

macro_segments.title: Improper Handling of Sefer Torah and Loss of Mitzvah Credit

micro_segments.title: Rav Parnach in name of Rabbi Yochanan: One who holds Sefer Torah without cover (naked) is buried without mitzvah
Clarification by Abaye: Loses credit for that specific mitzvah, not buried without any mitzvos
Tosafot: Some interpret as losing credit for holding; Others say you lose credit for all mitzvos done in that context (e.g., reading while touching klaf directly)

macro_segments.title: Final Teaching: Rolling Covers Before Sealing and Studying Laws According to Seasons

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yannai: Better to roll the covers than to roll the Torah itself
Rashi: Roll Torah before putting cover on, not after cover is already in place
Baraita: Moses established that Israel should ask and discuss the laws of each festival at its appropriate time
Teaching on ritual learning: Beyond practical knowledge, learning festival laws IS the way to embrace the nature of the holiday
Closing emphasis: The masechet ends not on reading scripture but on learning Torah - talmud Torah relating to each season

topical_tags.term: Rava Shefatya in name of Rabbi Yochanan
Sanctity of Torah margins and structures
Bas kol - heavenly voice as sign
Improper Torah handling and lost merit
Learning festival laws at appropriate seasons
